name: Document Title
purpose: Any title text at the very top of the document, such as 'Demand Letter against Local Union 28'.

name: Date
purpose: The date the demand letter is issued.

name: Delivery Method
purpose: The method of delivery specified at the top, such as 'BY E-MAIL'.

name: Recipient Block
purpose: The name, title, organization, and address of the opposing party or counsel receiving the letter.

name: Subject Line
purpose: The 'Re:' line providing a brief description of the legal claim and location.

name: Epigraph / Corporate Policy Quote
purpose: Any quoted corporate policy or prefatory text appearing before the salutation.

name: Salutation
purpose: The greeting to the recipient, such as 'Dear Mr. Starace,' or 'Dear Mr. Berry,'.

name: Introduction
purpose: Opening paragraphs identifying the representing law firm, the aggrieved client, and the core legal claims or historical context.

name: Factual Background
purpose: Unheaded narrative paragraphs detailing the initial incidents, context, or summary of the discriminatory or harassing conduct.

name: II. Inconsistencies with Findings
purpose: Specific section headed 'II. Inconsistencies with Findings' detailing the trial committee findings and discrepancies.

name: III. Lack of Termination shows room for Negligence Retention
purpose: Specific section headed 'III. Lack of Termination shows room for Negligence Retention' detailing the union's leniency and the client's police report.

name: Timeline of Discriminatory Conduct
purpose: Specific section headed 'Timeline of Discriminatory Conduct' detailing the day-by-day chronological events of the dispute.

name: Legal Violations
purpose: Section headed 'IV. Local Union 28’s Conduct Violates the Law' or 'JLR’s Conduct Violates the Law' detailing how the opposing party's conduct violates specific statutes.

name: Conclusion
purpose: Section headed 'IV. Conclusion' or 'Conclusion' stating the client's emotional distress, threat of imminent litigation, and potential for punitive damages.

name: Good Faith Settlement Offer
purpose: Paragraph stating that the letter is sent in a good faith effort to resolve the matter without litigation.

name: Spoliation / Preservation of Evidence Notice
purpose: Formal demand reminding the recipient to take immediate steps to preserve all relevant physical, electronic, and communicative records.

name: Call to Action / Deadline
purpose: Paragraph providing a specific deadline date by which the recipient must contact the firm to discuss an amicable resolution.

name: Signature Block
purpose: The sign-off (e.g., 'Yours truly,') and the printed name of the attorney.
